articles.title: Seven AAP MPs including Raghav Chadha, Ashok Mittal officially part of BJP as Rajya Sabha chair accepts merger
articles.description: Seven Aam Aadmi Party MPs have officially joined the Bharatiya Janata Party. Rajya Sabha Chairman CP Radhakrishnan approved their merger petition. This move significantly shifts the party numbers in the Upper House. The BJP's strength increases while AAP's reduces. Union Minister Kiren Rijiju welcomed the MPs. AAP has called the merger unconstitutional and sought clarification on record changes.

This follows the Delhi High Court dismissing his plea to recuse the judge. Kejriwal cited concerns over perceived bias. He believes the proceedings may not be fair. His move is a conscientious protest.

articles.title: Maharashtra Transport Minister calls meeting on mandatory Marathi for commercial passenger vehicle drivers
articles.description: Maharashtra Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik is making practical Marathi language knowledge compulsory for all auto-rickshaw and taxi drivers in the state. Action will be taken against those who do not comply with the new rule.

articles.title: Private credit fragility: India can reduce default risks by closing data gaps in India’s broader credit market

articles.title: Kavitha comes with her own TRS; will it damage father BRS' prospects
articles.description: K Kavitha has launched a new political party named TRS. This move reclaims the original name of her father K Chandrasekhar Rao's party, BRS. The name change from TRS to BRS was previously linked to the party's defeat. Kavitha's new party could rally BRS dissidents. Political analysts suggest it may impact BRS prospects and potentially the BJP.

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi working for BJP, says TMC leader Kunal Ghosh amid political row
articles.description: TMC leader Kunal Ghosh accused Rahul Gandhi of "working for the BJP" after the Congress MP criticized Mamata Banerjee and alleged corruption. Ghosh stated Gandhi failed to stop the BJP in other states and is now aiding them in West Bengal. The remarks followed Gandhi's criticism of both TMC and BJP over corruption and electoral malpractice.
